text.title: typescript-react-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for typescript-react-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Flipping noUncheckedIndexedAccess and reflexively asserting with ! on indexed access (e.g., array[0]!).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: noUncheckedIndexedAccess returns T | undefined from array[0]. The ! assertion suppresses the type check and reintroduces runtime crashes. Proper handling requires length checks or optional chaining.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'use client' to entire layouts to "make hooks work" instead of creating a thin client boundary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This flips the whole tree to client, losing Server Component benefits and increasing JavaScript sent to the browser.

failure-mode.description: Forgetting revalidatePath or revalidateTag after mutations in Server Actions. UI shows stale data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Next.js caches fetches by default. After a mutation, the cache must be invalidated, or the next fetch returns cached stale data.

failure-mode.description: Using useFormState (React 18 name) instead of useActionState (React 19). Hook will be removed in future versions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: useFormState is the legacy name. React 19 renamed it to useActionState. Using the old name targets code for removal.
